structure.title: Testing Events: lifespan and startup - shutdown - FastAPI
structure.text:
# Testing Events: lifespan and startup - shutdown - FastAPI

URL: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/testing-events/

Testing Events: lifespan and startup - shutdown¶
When you need lifespan
to run in your tests, you can use the TestClient
with a with
statement:
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
items = {}
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
items["foo"] = {"name": "Fighters"}
items["bar"] = {"name": "Tenders"}
yield

structure.title: clean up items
structure.text:
# clean up items
items.clear()
app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
@app.get("/items/{item_id}")
async def read_items(item_id: str):
return items[item_id]
def test_read_items():

structure.title: Before the lifespan starts, "items" is still empty
Inside the "with TestClient" block, the lifespan starts and items added
After the requests is done, the items are still there
structure.text:
# After the requests is done, the items are still there
assert items == {"foo": {"name": "Fighters"}, "bar": {"name": "Tenders"}}

structure.title: The end of the "with TestClient" block simulates terminating the app, so
structure.text: # The end of the "with TestClient" block simulates terminating the app, so

structure.title: the lifespan ends and items are cleaned up
